message:
fix(assign/test): expect 7 agent types in TestRankAgentsForTask ranking

Earlier in this batch the commit 947fedc6 "test(assign/capability):
extend TestDefaultCapabilities to cover all seven agent types"
extended the default agent type set from 3 to 7.  The
TestRankAgentsForTask test in effectiveness_test.go was still
asserting the old count of 3, so `go test ./...` now fails with:

    expected 3 rankings, got 7

at effectiveness_test.go:140.

internal/assign/effectiveness_test.go

- Update the assertion:
    if len(ranking.Rankings) != 3 {
        t.Errorf("expected 3 rankings, got %d", len(ranking.Rankings))
    }
  →
    if len(ranking.Rankings) != 7 {
        t.Errorf("expected 7 rankings, got %d", len(ranking.Rankings))
    }
  and update the inline comment accordingly.

- Adjust the downstream ranking-order assertions inside
  TestRankAgentsForTaskWithEffectivenessData to walk all 7
  entries and verify they're in descending-rank order instead
  of the old 3-element shape.

NOTE: the working tree also contains an apparent revert of
0c09978f "fix(coordinator,robot): enforce directory boundary in
wildcard glob matching" across internal/coordinator/conflicts.go
and internal/robot/synthesis.go.  Those two files are still
surfaced for operator review (see the earlier "SURFACE — ntm
conflicts.go / synthesis.go look like a revert" note) and are
intentionally not included in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
